page: Microsoft Azure E20_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 10 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores running at 2.6 GHz, with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes 35 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. The system is provisioned with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data positions the E20_v4 in the average tier across most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core performance score of 1412.69 and multi-core score of 18613.51 align with mid-range capabilities. Memory bandwidth tests show 1034.46 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 88.48 GB/sec for large uncached read blocks and 43.27 GB/sec for write operations. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression represents a relative bottleneck, scoring in the weak tier at 8.63 GB/sec, though multi-threaded decompression recovers to an average score of 66.24 GB/sec.

With its high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU, the E20_v4 is structured for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It achieves average performance in database operations with a score of 7170.89 on PassMark and Redis SET operations at 16.52 million operations per second. For machine learning, it processes 135M parameter LLM text generation at 96.24 tokens per second, but struggles with larger models, as shown by a weak prompt processing speed of 29.26 tokens per second on a 7B model. The instance is suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM relative to compute resources.
description: Microsoft Azure E20_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine configured with 20 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks consistently place the server in the average tier, with a PassMark CPU score of 19123.19 and a Redis SET throughput of 16.52 million operations per second. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized LLM prompt processing represent performance bottlenecks, scoring in the weak tier. The E20_v4 is designed for memory-intensive workloads, such as database operations, in-memory caching, and general-purpose enterprise applications that require a larger memory footprint relative to compute capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced mid-range performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine with 20 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of eight gigabytes per core for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz
160.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Average performance across database operations, Redis caching, and web serving
Suited for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, and enterprise applications
